content:
- Read the choose-task rules, refreshed `.ralph/current_tasks.md`, and checked `.ralph/current_tasks_done.md` plus story-35 task files.
- Confirmed there are exactly two active unblocked failing non-bug tasks, both in story 35, with task 01 already passing and no open bug outranking them.
- Verified the actual implementation stack is mixed Rust plus a Go `verifyservice`; the current active story work lives in `cockroachdb_molt/molt/verifyservice`, where `/jobs` already exists and task 02 is the cleanest immediate continuation of the completed multi-db config work.
- Next after quitting: execute `.ralph/tasks/story-35-verify-multi-database-config/02-task-verify-http-jobs-ux.md` using the `tdd` skill, then validate `make check`, `make test`, and `make lint` with Nix-backed commands as required by repo rules.

- Read previous Ralph progress plus `.ralph/current_task.txt`; current target is `.ralph/tasks/story-35-verify-multi-database-config/02-task-verify-http-jobs-ux.md`.
- Loaded the mandatory `improve-code-boundaries` and `tdd` skill instructions before touching code.
- Next: read the task file and story-local plan context to determine whether this turn must create/adjust a plan and quit, or execute against an existing `NOW EXECUTE` plan.

- Read story-35 task 02; it currently has no `<plan>` entry and no `NOW EXECUTE` marker, so this turn is planning-focused per Ralph rules.
- Checked the completed task 01 and its plan to align the new HTTP jobs UX work with the multi-database config boundary already introduced there.
- Next: inspect `cockroachdb_molt/molt/verifyservice` HTTP/job/runtime code and produce a task-02 plan that explicitly uses TDD slices plus one code-boundary flattening target.

- Inspected verify-service HTTP/job/runtime code and found the key task-02 refactor boundary: `Service` still stores only `activeJob` plus `lastCompletedJob`, while requests still use regex filters and job responses still expose a single monolithic `result`.
- Confirmed the existing multi-database resolver from task 01 can be reused as the selection/input boundary, but task 02 needs new glob-based request normalization, per-database job state, and a thread-safe in-memory job store behind the HTTP layer.
- Next: add the task-02 plan file, link it from the task markdown, and mark it `NOW EXECUTE` if the interface/types are coherent after this inspection.

- Created the task-02 plan at `.ralph/tasks/story-35-verify-multi-database-config/02-task-verify-http-jobs-ux_plans/2026-04-29-verify-http-jobs-ux-plan.md`.
- Linked that plan from `.ralph/tasks/story-35-verify-multi-database-config/02-task-verify-http-jobs-ux.md` and marked the plan `NOW EXECUTE` because the interface, boundary cleanup target, and TDD slices are coherent after code inspection.
- Next after quitting immediately: execute the plan in vertical red/green slices, replacing single-job storage with a thread-safe job store and moving the HTTP contract to the canonical per-database job schema, then run `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test`.

- Read saved progress plus the explicit `tdd` and `improve-code-boundaries` skill instructions.
- Confirmed current target remains story-35 task 02 and previous turn already created the task plan marked `NOW EXECUTE`.
- Next: read the task markdown and referenced plan in detail, then execute in vertical TDD slices against the planned interface.

- Read the task markdown and the linked task-02 execution plan; confirmed this turn should implement the planned HTTP/jobs refactor instead of creating a new plan.
- The plan centers on a code-boundary cleanup: separate request normalization, resolved execution planning, and a thread-safe job store from HTTP handlers.
- Next: inspect verifyservice code/tests at those seams, then start with the first RED test for `POST /jobs {}` returning the canonical multi-database job object.

- Inspected the current verifyservice seams: `Service` enforces one active job, `filter.go` still defines regex-based single-database requests, and `job.go` still returns top-level `result` / `failure`.
- Verified task-02 really is a breaking-contract refactor on the public HTTP layer, with config resolution available as the foundation for concrete configured database names.
- Next: inspect the existing HTTP test helpers fully, then replace the first POST/GET contract tests with a red tracer bullet for canonical multi-database job objects.

- Wrote the first RED tracer bullet in `verifyservice/http_test.go`: `POST /jobs {}` must return the canonical multi-database job object for all configured databases.
- Ran the focused test through `nix develop -c go test`; it failed as expected with HTTP 400 because the old implementation still rejects `{}` when multiple databases are configured.
- Next: replace the old request compilation and single-active-job start path with normalized multi-database planning plus canonical job responses.

- Replaced the verifyservice request boundary with glob-based normalization and config-backed resolution, plus a runner-facing resolved database request.
- Reworked service/job lifecycle around a thread-safe in-memory job store and canonical per-database job responses; updated verifyservice package tests and runtime tests to the new contract, then got `go test ./verifyservice` green under Nix.
- Next: run the repo-required gates (`make check`, `make lint`, `make test`), fix any cross-package fallout, then do the final task bookkeeping only if all required checks pass.
